article_meta.title: Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles 1 of 18 | Thousands of people protesting mas

reasoning: The model classifies this AP article as Center, which is defensible given the extremely minimal author prose. The article is essentially a photo gallery with brief factual captions. However, there are subtle issues: the confidence of 0.98 is arguably too high given that the article only presents anti-deportation protesters without any pro-enforcement perspective, which could be seen as a slight structural lean. The key_indicators are all from the author's own prose (photo captions and lead text), so no attribution violations. The classification is reasonable - AP wire reports on protests are typically Center or Lean Left, and this one has so little author prose that Center is defensible.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: The article is from AP and is a very sparse news report/photo gallery. Center is reasonable, though one could argue Lean Left given the exclusive focus on anti-deportation protesters with no pro-enforcement perspective. Center is within one adjacent class of a possible Lean Left classification.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.98 confidence is quite high. While the prose is indeed neutral and factual, the one-sided source selection (only covering anti-deportation protesters, no government or pro-enforcement voices) introduces some ambiguity about whether this is truly Center or Lean Left. A confidence of 0.75-0.85 would be more appropriate for an article with this structural consideration.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: The key_indicators cite the author's own prose and photo captions, no attribution violations. However, the last indicator is a photo caption which is borderline - it's the author/editor's descriptive choice but is somewhat formulaic. The reasoning correctly identifies the article as straight news with minimal evaluative language. Could have been stronger by noting the structural choice to cover only one side of the issue.
